meta_title: For Students: email automation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Email Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build email automation and overcome a lack of portfolio. An intermediate-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for developers.
content.answer_block: Transform your resume from academic to industry-ready by building a production-grade email automation system. By automating outreach or transactional workflows, you demonstrate the ability to integrate APIs, manage asynchronous tasks, and handle data—the exact skills hiring managers hunt for in junior developers struggling with a blank portfolio.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank space where your experience should be. The job market doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about what you've shipped. You feel the weight of the 'no experience' trap, watching peers land roles while your GitHub remains a ghost town of abandoned tutorials. It’s time to stop studying and start engineering.

We are going to bridge the gap between student and professional by building a robust email automation engine. This isn't a toy project; it's a real-world utility that solves a business problem. With Antigravity’s rapid scaffolding and Claude Code’s intelligent iteration, you will move from zero to a deployed, high-value asset in hours, not weeks.

steps.title: Define the Email Workflow Logic
steps.description: Establish the trigger-action sequence. Whether it's a newsletter sign-up or a cold outreach bot, define your JSON schema for the email payload.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Node.js project architecture for an email automation service that processes tasks from a queue using a modular controller-service pattern.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'init project structure with typescript and zod for schema validation'

steps.title: Integrate the SMTP Provider
steps.description: Connect to an email service like Resend or SendGrid. Focus on error handling and authentication security.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an email service provider module using the Resend API, including robust error handling for connection timeouts and rate limiting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'install resend sdk and implement email service with async/await error handling'

steps.title: Implement Asynchronous Task Queuing
steps.description: Use a queue to ensure your app doesn't crash under load. This is a critical enterprise-level practice.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup a BullMQ worker configuration to process email jobs asynchronously, ensuring retries on failure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate BullMQ with Redis and create a worker process for email queue'

steps.title: Build the Frontend Dashboard
steps.description: Create a simple UI to track email status and logs. This provides visual proof of your work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a React dashboard that fetches and displays the current email queue status and recent logs from the backend API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create dashboard UI with tanstack query for data fetching'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Deploy your app to a provider like Render or Railway and set up basic logging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Dockerfile and a deployment configuration script for a Node.js email microservice.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'optimize docker build for production and write deployment instructions'

pro_tips: Always implement rate-limiting at the API gateway level to prevent your service from being blacklisted by ESPs.
Use environment variables for all sensitive keys; never hardcode credentials, even in local development.
Write comprehensive integration tests for your email service; recruiters love seeing test coverage in a portfolio.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode that logs emails to the console instead of sending them, saving you money during testing.
Create a professional README.md that explains the 'why' behind your technical choices, not just the 'how'.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, add a feature to track email open rates using webhooks, then host the project on a custom domain to show it off on your portfolio site.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a developer. Your portfolio is a reflection of your ambition, and this project is the perfect starting point to prove your worth to potential employers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
